marketState: neutral / mixed - bearish near-term technicals and storage overhang vs a bullish medium-term weather and positioning setup
summary: NG front is at $2.90, sitting almost exactly on model fair value ($2.919, FAIR status at every horizon), so there is no standalone fair-value edge today. The medium-term case is more constructive: the 8-16 day weather outlook is running +31.2% above the 5-year norm and above its historical max range, which should lift cooling demand and is already pulling the fair-value curve higher (from $2.919 now to $2.999 in 21 days), while managed money is heavily net-short at just the 8th percentile of 5 years, raising squeeze risk. Against that, storage is +183 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish build surprise this week (+17 Bcf vs forecast), seasonality reliability is low this week (63pp spread), and technicals are bearish on 5 of 6 timeframes with several 50/200 death crosses. Near-term (1-7D) weather is only mildly above normal (+2.6%, mid-range), not extreme, so it doesn't yet override the bearish technical/storage backdrop for the next few days. Net read: lean long for the medium-term weather/squeeze setup, but respect the bearish short-term technical picture until the heat signal is closer to landing.

longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; trim ~half at TP1, trail the rest
longTerm.rationale: The 8-16D demand outlook is extreme (+31.2% vs 5y, above the historical max range) and the fair-value curve already reflects rising demand out to 21 days ($2.919 -> $2.999); combined with managed money at the 8th percentile of 5-year net-short positioning, there is real squeeze potential if the heat verifies and storage builds slow. Seasonality's 69% odds of higher prices in 1 and 4 weeks add mild support (September historically the strongest month), though reliability is flagged low this year.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage keeps printing bearish surprises (bigger builds than forecast), the 8-16D heat signal rolls over back toward normal, or front price closes and holds below $2.65, breaking the lower fair-value band zone.

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 0.5-1% account risk given tighter stop and low conviction
shortTerm.rationale: Near-term (1-7D) weather is only mildly above normal (+2.6% vs 5y, mid-range position), leaving technicals and the fresh bearish storage surprise (+17 Bcf vs forecast) the deciding vote for this horizon; trend and EMA cross (50<200) are bearish on 5m/1h/4h/1d/1w with MACD negative on several, and price offers no fair-value edge (status FAIR everywhere).
shortTerm.invalidation: A close back above $3.02, RSI turning up through 50 with a bullish MACD cross, or the extreme 8-16D heat signal pulling forward into the 1-7D window earlier than expected.

keyDrivers: 8-16D weather demand +31.2% vs 5y and above the historical max range - rising cooling demand ahead
Managed money net short at the 8th percentile of 5 years - crowded short, squeeze risk
Storage +183 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish build surprise this week (+17 Bcf vs forecast)
Price is FAIR vs model now at every horizon, but the fair-value curve drifts higher over the next 3 weeks
Technicals bearish (death crosses, negative MACD) on 5 of 6 timeframes from 5m to 1w
risks: 8-16D weather forecasts can shift quickly and the extreme heat signal may not verify
Crowded short could keep pressing the market lower instead of squeezing, extending the bearish move
Seasonality reliability is low this week (63pp 10-90% spread) and YTD prices are structurally weak vs median (-14.9% vs -9.6%), weakening the seasonal tailwind as a guide
